Findings.Title: Console login by root account from unusual location
Findings.Description: Root account console login detected from IP 185.220.101.34 (Tor exit node, Romania) without MFA at 23:42 UTC on 2026-04-02.

Findings.Title: IAM credentials from dev-server-01 used from external IP
Findings.Description: Credentials associated with EC2 instance i-0dev567890 (dev-server-01) were used from IP 104.28.55.201, which is outside the expected AWS network. API calls include ListBuckets, AssumeRole, and GetObject on sensitive S3 buckets.

Findings.Title: Unusual S3 data access pattern on cloudvault-client-docs
Findings.Description: An assumed role (CloudVaultAdminRole) downloaded 4 objects from s3://cloudvault-client-docs/clients/ in rapid succession (16 seconds). This bucket contains sensitive client financial documents. The access pattern is inconsistent with normal application behavior.

Findings.Title: Port probe on unprotected port 22 of dev-server-01
Findings.Description: EC2 instance i-0dev567890 (dev-server-01) has an unprotected port 22 (SSH) that is being probed from 14 different external IPs. Security group sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6dev allows inbound SSH from 0.0.0.0/0.

Findings.Title: Port probe on unprotected port 3389 of dev-server-01
Findings.Description: EC2 instance i-0dev567890 (dev-server-01) has an unprotected port 3389 (RDP) that is being probed. Security group sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f6dev allows inbound RDP from 0.0.0.0/0 — this rule was added on 2026-04-01 by dev-contractor-01.

Findings.Title: Console login without MFA by james.park
Findings.Description: IAM user james.park logged into the console without MFA from IP 98.45.172.92. This user has AdministratorAccess policy attached.

Findings.Title: Multiple failed console logins for intern-2026
Findings.Description: IAM user intern-2026 had 2 failed console login attempts before a successful login, all without MFA. Account has broader permissions than expected for an intern role.

Findings.Title: Root account access key created
Findings.Description: A new access key was created for the root account from IP 185.220.101.34. Root access keys should not exist — all programmatic access should use IAM users or roles.

Findings.Title: Unusual IAM reconnaissance by dev-contractor-01
Findings.Description: IAM user dev-contractor-01 called ListAttachedUserPolicies and ListBuckets in rapid succession, followed by GetBucketAcl on the sensitive cloudvault-client-docs bucket. This pattern is consistent with privilege enumeration.

Findings.Title: Unusual AssumeRole activity by dev-contractor-01
Findings.Description: IAM user dev-contractor-01 assumed CloudVaultAdminRole, which provides full administrative access. This user's normal permissions are scoped to development resources only. The role assumption occurred 3 minutes after IAM reconnaissance activity.

Findings.Title: SSH brute force attempts against dev-server-01
Findings.Description: EC2 instance i-0dev567890 is being targeted by SSH brute force attacks from 8 unique IPs. 247 failed login attempts detected in the past 24 hours. The instance's security group allows SSH from 0.0.0.0/0.

Findings.Title: EC2 instance queried cryptocurrency mining domain
Findings.Description: EC2 instance i-0dev567890 (dev-server-01) queried a domain associated with cryptocurrency mining (pool.minexmr.com). This could indicate the instance has been compromised and is being used for cryptomining.

Findings.Title: Possible DNS data exfiltration from dev-server-01
Findings.Description: EC2 instance i-0dev567890 is making DNS queries to a domain consistent with DNS tunneling techniques. Subdomain queries contain encoded data patterns suggesting data exfiltration via DNS.

Findings.Title: S3 bucket cloudvault-app-assets granted public access
Findings.Description: S3 bucket cloudvault-app-assets was configured with a bucket policy that allows public read access (Principal: *). Changed by priya.sharma.

Findings.Title: S3 account-level block public access is not fully enabled
Findings.Description: The S3 account-level block public access setting is not fully enabled. BlockPublicAcls and RestrictPublicBuckets are disabled, allowing individual buckets to be made public.

Findings.Title: Outbound port scan from web-prod-01
Findings.Description: EC2 instance i-0web111111 (web-prod-01) is performing an outbound port scan against internal IP addresses. This could indicate reconnaissance or a compromised instance.

Findings.Title: API call from unusual network by svc-backup-agent
Findings.Description: IAM user svc-backup-agent made API calls from an IP address associated with an unusual ASN. Investigation shows this is the internal NAT gateway IP — likely a false positive due to network configuration.

Findings.Title: S3 block public access disabled on cloudvault-app-assets
Findings.Description: S3 block public access was disabled on bucket cloudvault-app-assets by priya.sharma. This allows public access configurations on this specific bucket.

Findings.Title: Port probe on port 8443 of app-prod-01
Findings.Description: EC2 instance i-0app222222 (app-prod-01) received connection attempts on port 8443 from external IPs. This port is used by the internal API but should not be reachable from the internet.

Findings.Title: Console login from new IP by dana.chen
Findings.Description: IAM user dana.chen logged into the console from IP 72.34.55.198, which has not been previously associated with this user. MFA was used. Likely a new location (hotel, conference) — low risk with MFA.

Findings.Title: Port probe on port 443 of web-prod-01
Findings.Description: External IPs probing port 443 on web-prod-01. This is expected — port 443 is intentionally open for HTTPS traffic. Informational finding.

Findings.Title: Port probe on port 443 of web-prod-02
Findings.Description: External IPs probing port 443 on web-prod-02. Same as gd-021 — expected traffic on an intentionally open port.

Findings.Title: Port probe on port 80 of web-prod-01
Findings.Description: External IPs probing port 80 on web-prod-01. Expected — HTTP redirect to HTTPS.

Findings.Title: Port probe on port 80 of web-prod-02
Findings.Description: External IPs probing port 80 on web-prod-02. Same as gd-023.

Findings.Title: API calls from unusual ASN by priya.sharma
Findings.Description: IAM user priya.sharma made API calls from coffee shop WiFi (Starbucks guest network). Calls included DescribeInstances and DescribeSecurityGroups — normal DevOps work from an unusual network.

Findings.Title: API call from flagged IP by svc-deploy-pipeline
Findings.Description: Service account svc-deploy-pipeline made an UpdateService call from an IP on a threat intelligence list. Investigation shows this is a CI/CD runner IP that was incorrectly flagged — false positive.

Findings.Title: Port probe from 103.45.67.89 on dev-server-01 port 22
Findings.Description: Additional SSH probe from Chinese IP range.

Findings.Title: Port probe from 91.134.22.11 on dev-server-01 port 22
Findings.Description: SSH probe from French IP range.

Findings.Title: Port probe from 185.156.73.44 on dev-server-01 port 22
Findings.Description: SSH probe from Russian IP range.

Findings.Title: Port probe from 5.188.210.77 on dev-server-01 port 22
Findings.Description: SSH probe.

Findings.Title: Port probe from 45.155.205.233 on dev-server-01 port 22
Findings.Description: SSH probe.

Findings.Title: Port probe from 194.26.29.102 on dev-server-01 port 22
Findings.Description: SSH probe.

Findings.Title: Port probe from 141.98.10.55 on dev-server-01 port 22
Findings.Description: SSH probe.

Findings.Title: Port probe from 77.247.181.165 on dev-server-01 port 22
Findings.Description: SSH probe from Tor exit node.

Findings.Title: API call from unusual ASN by marcus.webb
Findings.Description: marcus.webb accessed console from mobile hotspot IP. Normal activity — he was working from home on a day the office WiFi was down.

Findings.Title: Anonymous access check on cloudvault-logs bucket
Findings.Description: Informational: anonymous access was checked but not granted on cloudvault-logs. No action needed.

Findings.Title: Port probe on finance-server-01 port 5432
Findings.Description: External probe on PostgreSQL port. Port is not actually exposed — security group blocks it. Noise.

Findings.Title: API call from VPN exit by svc-report-gen
Findings.Description: svc-report-gen API calls routed through VPN exit. Expected — Lambda functions route through NAT/VPN.

Findings.Title: Port 25 probe on web-prod-01
Findings.Description: SMTP port probe. Port is closed. Background internet noise.

Findings.Title: Port 3306 probe on app-prod-01
Findings.Description: MySQL port probe. Port is not exposed. Noise.

Findings.Title: API calls from airport WiFi by james.park
Findings.Description: james.park accessed console from DFW airport WiFi. No MFA (consistent with gd-006). Low severity but reinforces MFA enforcement need.

Findings.Title: Port 445 probe on dev-server-01
Findings.Description: SMB port probe. Port is not exposed but dev-server-01 continues to attract scanning due to open SSH/RDP.

Findings.Title: Port 8080 probe on web-prod-02
Findings.Description: HTTP-ALT port probe. Port is closed. Noise.

Findings.Title: API call from residential ISP by dana.chen
Findings.Description: dana.chen accessed from home ISP IP on a weekend. MFA used. Normal remote work.

Findings.Title: Port 1433 probe on finance-server-01
Findings.Description: MSSQL port probe on finance server. Port is closed. Noise, but finance-server-01 being probed is worth noting.

Findings.Title: Port 27017 probe on app-prod-01
Findings.Description: MongoDB port probe. Not running MongoDB. Noise.

Findings.Title: API call from hotel WiFi by dana.chen
Findings.Description: dana.chen accessed from hotel IP during a conference trip to Las Vegas. MFA used. Expected travel pattern.
